candidates.stmt:
I have proudly called Cranbrook home for 35 years and I am standing as your Reform UK candidate.

I have worked in the hospitality industry for twenty years and have an MA in Music. I run a multi-award-winning kitchen in the town, so understand the pressures facing local people and businesses.

I will stand up for families and farmers, cut needless red tape and waste, back policies that support local businesses and help them thrive, push for lower costs, fight overdevelopment, and always put local people first.

If you want change, vote Reform UK!

I live on Culverden Park with my family so this part of Tunbridge Wells matters to me - it’s my home.

I am a solicitor and have previously been a Borough Councillor, trustee and school governor so have the experience to represent our area.

My priorities if I am elected are:

- stopping over-development - the Lib Dems council’s new Local Plan will force thousands of houses on Tunbridge Wells. But there are no guarantees on water supply, infrastructure or public services. We need to put the existing community first and make sure we only allow development once the infrastructure is secured. We need to learn the lessons of the water crisis.

- protecting the Common - I support the fantastic work of the Friends of the Common. I’ve been disappointed the Council has not used its financial or other resources better to support the community campaign. We need to change this urgently to make sure the Common is protected for the town and future generations.

- cleaning up the town - in too many places, the town needs a tidy-up. Litter, grime, graffiti, weeds, damaged pavements, broken benches… We need to focus on the local environment to make it a better place for residents and visitors.

- tackling anti-social behaviour and crime - too many people do not feel safe in our town centre, on the Common or in parks. We need to change this - with more visible police, town centre wardens, better CCTV and improved lighting.

- value for money - under the Lib Dems, council tax has gone up by 12% since 2023, car parking has risen by up to 188% in the last year, and the cost of things like green waste bins and parking permits have risen by a lot more than inflation. This makes a difference with all the cost of living pressures we face currently. We need a council that genuinely focuses on the cost of living and value for money. Keeping council tax low is a real priority for me.

I am standing because I love this town and this part of Tunbridge Wells in particular. I hope you will suppor

BENENDEN – CRANBROOK – FRITTENDEN – GOUDHURST – HAWKHURST – SANDHURST - SISSINGHURST
Bringing experience, expertise, and working knowledge of living here to the whole division of Cranbrook.
Former Cabinet member for Environmental Services, I have been a key part of restoring and securing Council finances, seeking out infrastructure solutions for Hawkhurst and Sandhurst, tackling crime, helping those in need, and seizing opportunities to revive Tunbridge Wells town centre and, importantly, our rural areas with work in the Neighbourhood Development groups.
There are a wide range of projects that I have in the pipeline for our area that will come into effect over the next few years and I can't wait to see them into fruition.

Hello
My name is Rosanna and I have been a district and town Councillor in Broadstairs and before that, a town, district and county Councillor in Shropshire. I have the necessary experience to represent the residents in Hawkhurst, Sandhurst and Benenden and if elected, will do my very best  to work hard for all in our wonderful villages.

Only the Conservatives have the team, plan and local leadership needed to improve Hawkhurst, Sandhurst and Benenden.

1) NO to overdevelopment. Our Local area is under serious threat of overdevelopment.
We have the track record of fighting for infrastructure every step of the way.

2) Keeping your tax low. Local people are worried about the cost of living. And we're on your side. That's why we voted against the Liberal Democrat Budget which increased Council Tax.

3) Need to coordinate road works better
There is a lack of joined up thinking on road closures and utility works. I will fight to make sure we have a sensible timetable for works and work closely with Councillor Bev Palmer and Councillor Tom Dawlings, as well as with Councillor Claudine Russell, our KCC Councillor.

4) Being local matters. In a Local Election, you have to have a local candidate.
I live in Hawkhurst and know about the challenges we face in all our villages.

5) Fixing potholes. Potholes are blighting our roads. They have become worse over the past year. 
Please sign our petition by going to fillkentpotholes.com

We have been delivering for our local area.
We work hard, all year round, not just at election time!

I am standing as your Reform UK candidate for the Hawkhurst, Sandhurst & Benenden ward in the Tunbridge Wells Borough Council election on the 7th May 2026.

I have worked and lived in Tunbridge Wells for nearly 25 years and want to give back to the community that has given me so much.

We all know that our country is in trouble and we are in desperate need of proper leadership which none of the mainstream political parties can offer us.

Reform UK took control of Kent County Council last year, and in less than a year, we have identified millions in savings and fixed the 2026/27 council tax increase at 3.99%, which is lower than the national 4.99% cap, and the lowest in Kent for many years.  I want the opportunity to bring those sorts of reforms to Tunbridge Wells Borough Council as your councillor.

I have been a supporter of Reform UK since it started and am proud to have campaigned and stood for election in previous Tunbridge Wells Borough Council and Kent County Council elections in 2024 and 2025.

I feel passionately about standing up for local businesses, making sure parents can find a place for their children at primary and secondary schools that are not miles away from where they live, and challenging the government’s unrealistic house building targets that will do nothing to provide affordable housing for local people, but will risk overwhelming our local infrastructure and destroying our countryside.

Remember, this year Reform UK successfully legally challenged the government’s plans to cancel local elections across much of the country, and thanks to our party, approximately 4.6 million people have had their right to vote restored in the local elections.

I am asking you for your vote so that I may have the chance to serve Hawkhurst, Sandhurst & Benenden.

"Hello, I’m Mike Appelbe, the 2023 Lib Dem candidate for Hawkhurst & Sandhurst.

You have voted me in to serve you on Hawkhurst Parish Council since 2008, and have re-elected me each time I have stood. Thank you. It has been reassuring for me that you think I have been doing the right thing. Service and duty are important to me.

I have always understood that our representation on planning matters is the most important role we, your elected representatives, have--and this has become increasingly obvious most recently.

After a long career working in IT the most important thing to me now is putting something back into the community I love and where I have lived for five decades. Please vote for me on May 4. " [taken from: https://sites.google.com/view/libdem-hawkhurst-sandhurst/home]

I am standing for Pantiles ward because I care deeply about Tunbridge Wells and want to play a more active role in shaping its future. Through my work with the Green Weald Alliance and local residents, I have already been engaging with key issues affecting our community, from road safety along Frant Road to the proposed development at St Mark’s and the concerns around Ramslye Farm. I have also been following local issues such as the future of the Colonnade and how it is managed for residents and businesses. I have been acting in this space because these issues matter, but I am now seeking a clear mandate to do more and to represent residents formally.

We need development that is responsible, sustainable, and genuinely aligned with local needs. Building executive homes on green spaces does not solve affordability challenges or infrastructure pressures, and it risks damaging the character and heritage of our area, including important historical assets. I will advocate for a brownfield and vacant homes first approach, protect our green spaces, and ensure that residents have a strong, credible voice in the decisions that affect them.

As a Conservative, I am championing the Tunbridge Wells First campaign.

We face the most important point for Tunbridge Wells for many years. The Borough Council, will soon be abolished and a new West Kent or ‘all of’ Kent’ Unitary Authority created. I am campaigning for all discretionary services, local assets and appropriate services are assigned to a new Royal Tunbridge Wells Town Council. The Borough Council, with its existing Leaders, is more concerned with their position the West Kent. In other Boroughs, councils are signing letters of intent to allocate these assets, revenue and local services to their main towns. Our Council has been negative, claiming only allotments are guaranteed for inclusion by a new RTW town council.

As Dep. Chairman of the (non-political) RTW Town Forum, I did the detailed work (later confirmed by the Council) that showed we only receive £1.4 million is services specific to out town while RTW residents pay £2.8m in extra tax which should be earmarked for our town. This includes payments for our parks, the Common, community space and other discretionary services.

The strong finances and low council taxes of the Borough of Tunbridge Wells is based on the many assets accumulated over the last 30 years or primarily Conservative councils. Without the Borough’s ownership on the car parks, the RVP shopping plaza and many other assets, our taxes would be much higher. We would also not had the opportunity to buy the RVP lease without the foresight of Conservative Councils. We stand to loose so much if we allow all of these to be absorbed into the new Unitary Authority. They should be retained by a new Town Council.

I my tenure as Deputy Leader of the Borough Council during COVID, we helped ensure we had a positive accounting financial result throughout. I also set up and led the cross party investigation into the Town Hall. This committed, and achieved a unanimous agreement to transition it to shared commercial office and work space. I work

I am standing for election as the Alliance candidate in Park so that I can continue to work hard for residents as a local, independent councillor, free from the influence of a national political party.

Why vote for me?

- I was part of the team that delivered a community funded playground in Calverley Grounds.

- I challenged the proposed development on the edge of Calverley Grounds for a new town hall, office block, underground car park and theatre. After several years, this project was cancelled. The development would have cost taxpayers at least £108 million, and was likely to exceed £150 million.

- I pushed for the unused space in the Town Hall to be rented out from 2017. The Town Hall is now a thriving building with many small businesses using the building alongside Tunbridge Wells Borough Council.

- I worked hard to save several bus routes serving Hawkenbury and Tunbridge Wells.

- My wife and I launched and organised the annual Town Wide Yard sale since 2017.

- I have helped residents pull together to challenge rogue builders.

- Now, I challenge the spending of £68 million to redevelop part of Royal Victoria Place. The cost is likely to rise above £100 million. There are much lower cost and effective plans that could bring life back into Ely Court and the old food court in the shopping centre.

- I will push for Royal Tunbridge Wells to get a Town Council and work to ensure it gets more than the default set of assets (the allotments) in the local government restructuring.

Since 2019 it has been my privilege to serve as your councillor for Pembury and now Capel. 
During that time, I have worked independently and consistently to put local residents first, not party politics. I alone in our ward voted against the, ruinous for Pembury and Capel, Local Plan.

I am a recently retired NHS Consultant who has lived in Tunbridge Wells for ten years. After a career serving the public as a doctor, I now have the time to work for you as your representative on Tunbridge Wells Council. I will bring my experience from over 40 years as a doctor working with people from all backgrounds to represent you and take up your concerns.

I will ensure that council policies support all residents but especially those who need the services most. 

I have managed budgets in the face of severe financial constraints and will ensure that your council tax is spent wisely. 

I will oppose the current anti-car policies, keep car parking charges down, so that our local businesses can attract more trade and allow free parking at our parks.

1. Protecting our rural ways of life and supporting local businesses
2. Fighting overdevelopment and the lack of enforcement where planning has been refused
3. Lobbying KCC with our pothole petition to make our rural roads safer
4. Campaign to stop the Farmers and family Tax, and the labour governments attack on our rural communities
5. Protect our local environment and support polices that cut carbon emissions in ways that are sustainable for our residents
6. Campaign to help relieve parking, traffic issues as well as improve bus routes and road safety in our villages

I was fortunate to move back to the area four years ago and I really appreciate how beautiful the landscape is and how important the local communities are. Having worked in the NHS for over a decade I have seen how societal injustice has had a huge negative impact on the nation's health. The Green Party's vision of a healthy society for all is one that I fully share; it means good quality housing, clean water and material security for all, accessible public services and a clean, safe environment. I am a keen supporter of local initiatives to promote dark skies, create new habitats and re-introduce native species.

I have lived in the borough for over 20 years and have been campaigning locally on environmental issues since 2006. I love living here between London and the coast with access to so much countryside, history and culture. I work from home managing community support services for people living with dementia. I believe in the power of communities to make life better for everyone, now and into the future. I am a passionate advocate for community energy - harnessing the power of renewable energy for the local community - and promoting better home insulation and retrofitting older homes to reduce our carbon footprint and help with fuel poverty - no one should have to choose between heating and eating.

I would like to introduce myself as the Conservative candidate for the Local Elections in May for the Tunbridge Wells West division, which includes Bidborough (where I live), Speldhurst, Langton Green, Ashurst, Groombridge, Rusthall and Culverden.  I have lived in this part of Kent for over 30 years.

I am a solicitor, working from home with my wife Tina. Our children attended school in Tonbridge. I have experience in Local Government, having been a Kent County Councillor before, which means that I know my way around KCC services.

I served in the Army (Regular and Reserve) and was until recently Commandant of Kent Army Cadet Force, which does great work with young people in Tunbridge Wells and across Kent. I was also KCC’s Armed Forces champion, helping to ensure that veterans, Armed Forces families and servicemen and women can access County services. During my time we led KCC to achieve the Armed Forces Covenant Gold Award, which meant real changes to the way the KCC worked for the Services Community.

If elected I will work for all residents of this area:
•	 to get value for our taxes out of the County Council. 
•	To minimise the damage being done by Labour to our economy. 
•	To hold the Liberal Democrat run Borough Council to account.
•	To support the good work done by Parish Councils and to help any resident, whether they voted for me or not, when they have a problem with County services.
